item.title: Fergusson islanders demand compensation for 'unauthorised FCA logging'
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities on Fergusson Island are demanding compensation for environmental damage and unpaid royalties from logging operations they say were carried out without their consent.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Port Moresby’s newest shopping mall opens its doors
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The opening of the Eliseo Premium, a five-storey retail and entertainment complex in Hohola, reflects growing business confidence in Papua New Guinea’s capital. The post Port Moresby’s newest shopping mall opens its doors appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: Ryan Ani: From Raukele to the Barramandis
item.description: [Inside PNG] A fresh face is joining the PNG Barramandis as they head to Japan for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific (EAP) Qualifier. At just 22 years old, Ryan Ani is ready to make his international debut. Ani, who hails from Hula and Babaka in the Rigo Coastal area of Central Province, grew up [...

item.title: PNG Pageant Figure Tyla Singirok Condemns Online Abuse
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Tyla Krystal Singirok has condemned cyber harassment directed at her following her recent exit from the Miss Bird of Paradise PNG organisation, saying online users must learn to act with accountability and decency. The former inaugural Miss Bird of Paradise PNG titleholder issued a statement after f

item.title: Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea plans to phase out raw log exports by 2025, promoting local processing to boost economic growth, job creation, and sustainable forest management, potentially increasing forestry revenue significantly. The post Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits first appeare

item.title: PNG50 Gospel Music Festival set for August
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Jubilee Reset Festival: A Nation Gathers to Worship, Pray, and Reset for the Future As Papua New Guinea approaches its 50th year of Independence, a powerful wave of faith and unity is building across the nation. At the heart of this movement is the Jubilee Reset Gospel Music Festival, taking place o

item.title: PNG Government extends fuel relief as global prices continue to climb
item.description: [PNG Facts] Thousands of motorists and businesses across PNG have been spared another rise in fuel prices after the government’s subsidy program continued to absorb increasing import costs this month. Without the assistance package, fuel prices were expected to jump again as international oil markets remained v

item.title: Over 450 pets in NCD receive free desexing through RSPCA and VBB outreach
item.description: [Post Courier] The Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals of Papua New Guinea (RSPCA PNG), in partnership with Vets Beyond Borders (VBB) Australia, has concluded a highly successful 16-day mass desexing drive in the National Capital District. The outreach, which operated across seven different loca

item.title: Barakau Primary School Sets New Standard in Teacher Welfare with Staff Uniform Initiative
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Barakau Primary School in the Central Province has taken a significant step towards improving teacher welfare and boosting staff morale with the presentation of new staff uniforms to its teachers. The initiative, led by newly appointed head teacher Mr. Stephen Hare, has been widely praised by staff

item.title: Fraud Squad cracks K2.1 million corporate scam – key suspect arrested
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Police have arrested and charged a former employee of a finance company over a coordinated internal fraud scheme that is alleged to have stolen close to K2.1 million through forged invoices, inflated quotations and collusion with accomplices. “The National Fraud and Anticorruption Directorate in

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: Bougainville landowners warn companies at Panguna mine to stop work
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A group of landowners in the area of the Panguna mine in Bougainville has issued a public warning to companies trying to redevelop the mine to stop work and leave.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: Moni Plus MD on standing out in Papua New Guinea’s competitive financial services market
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Financial institution Heduru Moni Limited, trading as Moni Plus, has been on an impressive growth trajectory in recent years. In this exclusive interview with Business Advantage PNG , its Managing Director Aho Baliki expands on its long-term strategy and shares what it is doing to stand out in Papua

item.title: USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation
item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG icon Alex Johnston signs with PNG Chiefs for 2028. The NRL try-scoring king joins as the first local hero, honoring his proud Kumuls heritage. The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG C

item.title: Marape slams ‘shallow’ critics of PNG NRL bid
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PRIME Minister James Marape has hit back at detractors of the PNG NRL bid, labeling those who dismiss the move as "shallow-minded" and unable to grasp the massive economic and social windfall the franchise will bring to the country. As Papua New Guinea prepares to celebrate its 50th year of Independ

item.title: Volcanic Lakes in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's volcanic lakes, formed by ancient eruptions, offer tourism opportunities and support local economies and ecosystems. Sustainable management can enhance eco-tourism while preserving these unique natural treasures. The post Volcanic Lakes in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG NRL Competition finalise Club Line-Up for 2025 Season
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The PNG NRL Competition has announced that the 12 existing teams will compete for the top spot in the 2025 Digicel ExxonMobil Cup, starting in April of 2025. After thoroughly reviewing the 2024 season and assessing the potential for expansion, it was concluded that the competition in 2025 will featu

item.title: PNG targets clean energy transport shift by 2035
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea is setting its sights on a cleaner energy future, with the Government planning a nationwide move away from fossil fuels towards renewable-powered transport and energy systems over the next 10 years. PNG targets clean energy transport shift by 2035 The announcement was made by Prime

item.title: Tony Ura’s blistering century powers PNG to 136-run rout of South Korea
item.description: [Post Courier] Opening batsman Tony Ura put on a masterclass at Korogi Sports Park on Saturday, smashing a sensational unbeaten century to lead the Papua New Guinea Barramundis to a crushing 136-run victory over South Korea in the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup East Asia Pacific Qualifier. Opting to bat first, the Barram

item.title: Over 100 children and five pregnant mothers vaccinated in Dorobisoro
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] MORE than 100 children and pregnant mothers got vaccinated today at Dorobisoro village, Central Province. Men and women walked two to three hours from neighboring villages with their children and received vaccines and supplies of medicines. Community Health Worker Elizah Lofulo said a total of 105 p

item.title: PM Marape commends Lihir Gold for strong Tax contribution, urges responsible use of Public Funds
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has commended Lihir Gold Limited for its substantial tax contribution to the National Government, describing it as a strong reflection of improving compliance in Papua New Guinea’s resource sector amid high global gold prices. The Prime Minister confirmed that Lihir Gold

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding one of Papua New Guinea’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: Papua New Guinea banking enters its digital age
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's banks are scaling up digital platforms to fuel growth and advance financial inclusion, as CEOs in the sector tell Business Advantage PNG . The post Papua New Guinea banking enters its digital age appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank backs the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards, sponsoring the Small Business category to drive growth and recognize local creative entrepreneurs. The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 ap

item.title: PNG Chiefs Land Try-Scoring King Alex Johnston for 2028
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea’s push to establish a powerhouse rugby league outfit has gained fresh momentum, with PNG Kumul representative Alex Johnston confirmed to join the PNG Chiefs in 2028. The announcement delivers a major boost for the code in PNG, with Johnston bringing a wealth of experience and a prov

item.title: Know Your Rights – Absolute & Qualified
item.description: [Pawa TV] The PNG Constitution protects both absolute and qualified rights, with absolute rights being inviolable. Qualified rights can be restricted by law, raising concerns about accountability and governance, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. The post Know Your Rights – Absolute & Qualified first ap

item.title: Operation Render Safe set to begin in East New Britain
item.description: [PNG Buzz] This week, Governor Michael Marum and Provincial Administrator Levi Mano welcomed Australian High Commissioner John Feakes, Defence Adviser Colonel Travis Gordon, and a team from the Australian Defence Force (ADF) in East New Britain. This visit marks the beginning of a series to the province, as th

item.title: PNG Former Miss Bird of Paradise Queen Tyla Singirok Announces Departure
item.description: [PNG Facts] Former PNG inaugural Miss Bird of Paradise winner Tyla Krystal Singirok has officially bowed out from the Miss Bird of Paradise PNG platform, ending her involvement with the organisation after serving both on stage and behind the scenes. In a statement shared publicly, Ms Singirok spoke about the em

item.title: NDDA stands Firm on Bialla as Nakanai District headquarters
item.description: [Post Courier] The Nakanai District Development Authority (NDDA) Board has officially defended its choice of Bialla as the district's permanent headquarters, responding to recent public debate and concerns raised by residents of the Hoskins LLG. In a formal statement, the Board clarified that the selection was not

item.title: 50-year wait ends: New road opens for 2,000 cocoa farmers in North Bougainville
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By WASITA ROYAL After more than 50 years without a proper road, the Tehobuin Sipotavai Road has been officially opened, giving 2,000 people in North Bougainville all-weather access to markets, hospitals, and schools for the first time. The 6.8-kilometre rehabilitation project was opened on 29 April

item.title: PNG PM welcomes Pacific Delegates, calls for regional shift to Clean Energy and Electric Transport
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has welcomed Pacific leaders, ministers, delegates, and development partners to Port Moresby for the 6th Pacific Regional Energy and Transport Ministers’ Meeting (PRETMM 6), while calling for a united regional transition towards clean energy and electricity-based transpo

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding the KK Connections corporate group, one of PNG’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Loloata unveils new food offering
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Mary Tao investigates the new food offerings at Loloata Island Resort under their new head chef. The post Loloata unveils new food offering appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony
item.description: [Education News PNG] More than 60 outstanding students and graduates of The University of the South Pacific (USP) were recognised for academic excellence at the Medals and Prizes The post USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank reopens its upgraded Rangeview Digital Hub on Level 1, bringing instant cards, loans, and digital support to Port Moresby shoppers. The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with

item.title: PNG Government Prioritises Stability and Electoral Integrity Before 2027 Election
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea’s national leadership is placing renewed emphasis on governance performance and institutional credibility as the country prepares for the 2027 National General Election, Prime Minister James Marape says. He stressed that delivering essential services remains the Government’s main co

item.title: The Challenge of Maritime Security
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's maritime security is crucial due to its rich marine resources and threats from illegal fishing. Enhanced surveillance, training, and international cooperation are vital for protecting these waters. The post The Challenge of Maritime Security first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Delegation learns from Madagascar’s Vanilla Industry
item.description: [PNG Buzz] A delegation from Papua New Guinea, including senior officers from central government agencies such as the Department of National Planning and Monitoring (DNPM), the Spice Industry Board (SIB) under the Department of Agriculture and Livestock (DAL), as well as Provincial Government and Administratio

item.title: PNG Eyes Stronger Trade and Investment Ties with United States
item.description: [PNG Facts] PRIME Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea wants deeper economic and strategic cooperation with the United States as senior officials from both nations meet in Port Moresby for high-level bilateral talks. Speaking during the opening of the second PNG–United States Strategic Partnership Dialog

item.title: Environment Minister orders urgent probe into alleged missing CCDA Green Fee funds
item.description: [Post Courier] Minister for Environment, Conservation, and Climate Change, Simo Kilepa, has ordered an immediate investigation into allegations regarding missing revenue from the Green Fee collection system at Jacksons International Airport. The government's action follows a Post-Courier Online exclusive , which b

item.title: Mangi Last Percy donates jersey to SHSS Norths Panga
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO Jeffery Mol, through LUCKYBET777 has donated a set of jerseys to the SHSS Norths Panga team at UPNG, Waigani Campus. Widely known through his content-creating Facebook page “Mangi Last Percy,” Mr. Mol has carried out several community support initiatives this year with the backing

item.title: PM Marape visits Energy Giants of China with proposal for PNG to become energy supplier, manufacturing base
item.description: [The PNG Sun] On the final day of his working visit to China, PNG Prime Minister, James Marape visited energy giants Shenzhen Energy and BYD to explore collaboration in green energy and enhance energy security. The Prime Minister’s visit was driven by his vision to transition Papua New Guinea (PNG) into a clean e

item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] PNGEC i wari long hevi bilong sekyuriti na i surukim bai-ileksen bilong Dei Open i go bek tupela de

item.title: ACT NOW! backs Parliamentary Committee call for immediate and drastic reform in forest sector
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! supports the call from the Special Parliamentary Committee on Public Sector Reform for ‘immediate and nonnegotiable structural, punitive and legislative reforms’ in the forestry sector.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process
item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.title: People Moves: Datec PNG, Kina Bank, BSP, Pacific Palms Property, PNG Ports, Business Council of PNG
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] CEO appointed at Datec PNG, new Deputy Chair for Kina Bank, key appointments at BSP, new head of property development at Steamships' Pacific Palms Property, board appointments at PNG Ports and Business Council of PNG. The post People Moves: Datec PNG, Kina Bank, BSP, Pacific Palms Property, PNG Port

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: PNG PM Defends China Trip, Cites Trade Gains
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has returned to Port Moresby describing his recent visit to Guangdong Province in China as productive, rejecting criticism that the trip was unnecessary travel. Mr Marape said the three-day visit delivered outcomes across key sectors, including mining, fo

item.title: King’s Baton Relay 2026
item.description: [Pawa TV] The King’s Baton Relay, starting in 1958, symbolises Commonwealth unity and values. The 2026 relay, the longest and most inclusive yet, features personalised batons for each nation, fostering cultural celebration and community spirit. The post King’s Baton Relay 2026 first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Bougainville chocolate festival 2023 underway
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The 2023 annual Bougainville Chocolate Festival is now underway, held over three days from the 26 to 28 September at the Hutjena Oval in Buka. It will be the largest event since the inaugural festival in 2016. The theme of this year’s festival is “Innovation and Information Sharing to Empower Farmer

item.title: PNG Pushes Women Into Politics with Mandatory Party Quota
item.description: [PNG Facts] WITH the 2027 National General Election approaching, Papua New Guinea has introduced a legal requirement compelling political parties to include women among their endorsed candidates. The new provision, announced by Integrity of Political Parties and Candidates Commission Registrar Emmanuel Pok, set

item.title: BSP Life pays K100,000 Life Insurance benefit to grieving family
item.description: [Post Courier] The family of the late Marina Joyce Ken, an employee of Datec PNG Limited at the time of her passing, has received a K100,000 life insurance benefit from BSP Life, following her passing on 4 April 2026. Late Ms Ken passed away at the young age of 25, following a courageous battle with cancer. To acc

item.title: 4,000 locals get Starlink connection in remote Dorobisoro
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] A REMOTE village in Papua New Guinea received full internet access through Starlink connection this week in Dorobisoro village, inland Rigo in Central Province. It wasn’t just connectivity, but a struggle needed on Thursday. A dawn of opportunities for the locals to connect to the outside world thro

item.title: PM Marape welcomes scrutiny, orders full review of Defense transaction while rejecting premature conclusions
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has welcomed public scrutiny surrounding the reported K20 million defense transaction, while cautioning against premature and misleading conclusions drawn without full facts. Responding to concerns raised by Opposition Leader Hon. James Nomane, the Prime Minister acknowl

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: ACT NOW! backs Parliamentary Committee call for immediate and drastic forest sector reform
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! supports the call from the Special Parliamentary Committee on Public sector Reform for ‘immediate and nonnegotiable structural, punitive and legislative reforms’ in the forestry sector.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.title: World Bank country head explains new six-year partnership with PNG
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In February, the World Bank Group approved a US$1.2 billion financing commitment to support Papua New Guinea’s development goals. In this interview with Business Advantage PNG , its new country head Han Fraeters reveals which sectors will receive the most investment and shares why he believes the pr

item.title: Term 2 Delay in Manus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend
item.description: [Inside PNG] Christchurch's Super Round rebirth will give rugby a timely boost as rival destinations, including Fiji, jostle for a piece of the action. The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend

item.title: PNG Commission Raises Red Flags Over Starlink Decision
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The Ombudsman Commission in Papua New Guinea is standing firm on its position regarding the Starlink case, with Chief Ombudsman Richard Pagen confirming plans to escalate the matter to the National Government. During a gathering in Kimbe, Mr Pagen said the Commission continues to question aspects of

item.title: Community Health Workers in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Community health workers (CHWs) are vital in Papua New Guinea's rural health care, offering essential services, promoting health education, and bridging gaps despite facing challenges like limited resources and training opportunities. The post Community Health Workers in PNG first appeared on PAWA T

item.title: Agripreneurs empowered, MSMEs boosted in Sepik Region
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Under the EU-STREIT PNG programme, FAO equips local entrepreneurs with knowledge, skills and practical tools needed to drive inclusive sustainable development for MSMEs in the Sepik region. Wewak, Papua New Guinea – The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), as part of the EU

item.title: PNG NRL Push Linked to Jobs, Tourism and National Identity, says Marape
item.description: [PNG Facts] Prime Minister James Marape has linked Papua New Guinea’s NRL franchise bid to wider economic and social gains, saying the proposed PNG Chiefs team will drive tourism, create jobs and strengthen national identity. PNG NRL Push Linked to Jobs, Tourism and National Identity, says Marape He said the Go

item.title: Bible Faith Outreach Orphanage launches Christian home schooling program
item.description: [Post Courier] After 20 years of dedicated service, the Bible Faith Outreach Orphanage Centre in Dobel, Mt Hagen, has introduced a home schooling program for the homeless and disadvantaged children under its care. According to caregiver Rosa Kepo, the centre has adopted the Accelerated Christian Education (ACE) cu

item.title: McBRO finance supports church project with K100,000
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO McBRO Finance has contributed K100,000 towards the construction of a new church building at Sinsibai Sacred Heart Parish in Upper Nebilyer, Tambul-Nebilyer District, Western Highlands Province. The locally owned financial company presented the cheque in Port Moresby this week as pa

item.title: PNG Chiefs secures second rugby league star Alex Johnston for 2028 debut
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The PNG Chiefs have secured a major signing ahead of their 2028 NRL entry, confirming South Sydney Rabbitohs winger Alex Johnston as their second inaugural player. General Manager for Football Michael Chammas made the announcement today, describing Johnston as one of the greatest players in rugby le

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Parliament Committee highly critical of PNG Forest Authority and the logging industry
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Special Parliamentary Committee on Public Sector Reform has published a report that is scathing in its criticism of the PNG Forest Authority and the logging industry.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: Inside Steamships’ property investment ‘master plan’
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Steamships continues to invest heavily in the Portside Business Park development, while also eyeing opportunities in other areas of Papua New Guinea such as Mount Hagen and Lae, as the head of its property division, Alan Heyns, tells Business Advantage PNG . The post Inside Steamships’ property inve

item.title: USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has today announced a $F1 million student hardship and bursary support package to protect students and their families The post USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’
item.description: [Inside PNG] More than half of Australians say they are giving up on regular hobbies such as gardening and playing sport due to stress brought on by extreme weather. The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’ first appeared on Inside PNG . The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-a

item.title: Starlink Goes Live in PNG, Opening Doors for Rural Connectivity
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has taken a major step toward closing its digital divide with the official launch of Starlink satellite internet services, now accessible across the country. Starlink Goes Live in PNG, Opening Doors for Rural Connectivity In a public notice, the National Information and Communicatio

item.title: Freshwater Fish Farming
item.description: [Pawa TV] Aquaculture in Papua New Guinea is rapidly expanding, enhancing food security and incomes through community-supported fish farming. While challenges persist, initiatives aim to cultivate a sustainable industry for rural development. The post Freshwater Fish Farming first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Showcasing PNG to the world: 2023 Goroka Show a Success
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Papua New Guinea’s rich cultural heritage came alive once again as the 67th Goroka Show took centre stage at the National Sports Institute (NSI) in Goroka, Eastern Highlands Province. Held over the long weekend commemorating PNG’s 48th Independence, this year’s event, sponsored by the National Gamin

item.title: Feo Announces New Medical University Investments to Tackle PNG Health Crisis
item.description: [PNG Facts] Minister for Higher Education Kinoka Feo has announced major government investments into Papua New Guinea’s medical training sector as part of efforts to address critical shortages in the country’s health workforce. Speaking at the commemoration of the International Day of the Midwife 2026, Minister

item.title: Roads and agriculture must grow together, says Prime Minister Marape
item.description: [Post Courier] Prime Minister James Marape has described the growing connection between road infrastructure, agriculture, and local economic participation in New Britain as a “powerful model” for the rest of Papua New Guinea. Speaking after travelling the improved corridor linking Kimbe in West New Britain to Koko

item.title: Prime Minister James Marape Arrives in Biala, Nakanai District, WNBP
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister Hon James Marape has just arrived in Bialla in the Nakanai District of West New Britain province to officially launch the first 10km of the 42km Bamus to Noahu road section of the New Britain Highway under the Connect PNG Program. The Prime Minister is being accompanied by the Ministe

item.title: PNGDF K20m investment sparks money laundering fears amid FATF Grey Listing: Opposition MPs
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Parliamentary opposition team has intensified allegations of potential money laundering following the Papua New Guinea Defense Force’s (PNGDF) decision to invest K20 million into the state-owned National Banking Corporation, warning the move could further damage the country’s standing with globa

item.title: K30.9 bilien baset
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavman i tokaut long K30.9 bilien baset bilong kantri long 2026 tasol em bai no inap yet long bungim ol nid na laik bilong ol manmeri.

item.title: Forestry Sector Money Laundering Risk Assessment presents a devastating picture of corruption
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A Money Laundering Risk Assessment for the Forestry Sector published by the Bank of Papua New Guinea presents a devastating picture of large-scale corruption, fraud and illegality in the logging industry.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Investing in Papua New Guinea: five things to watch in 2026
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In what is shaping up to be a big year for the Papua New Guinea economy, Business Advantage PNG lists five key focus areas to look out for in the country's investment space in 2026. The post Investing in Papua New Guinea: five things to watch in 2026 appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water
item.description: [Education News PNG] As the world marks World Water Day 2026 under the theme “Water and Gender Equality,” research from The University of the South Pacific (USP) has The post USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme
item.description: [Inside PNG] Ok Tedi Mining Limited welcomes 44 graduates into its 2026 development program, including seven top scholars from Unitech for a future-ready PNG. The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Developm

item.title: PNG Hunters outclassed at home as Falcons run riot in Hostplus Cup
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The PNG Hunters were overwhelmed on home soil after going down 28-8 to the Sunshine Coast Falcons in their Hostplus Cup clash in Port Moresby. PNG Hunters outclassed at home as Falcons run riot in Hostplus Cup In front of a home crowd at Santos National Football Stadium, the Hunters struggled to con

item.title: Table Tennis
item.description: [Pawa TV] Table tennis is an underrated sport in Papua New Guinea, steadily gaining recognition through impressive performances in regional competitions, including a historic silver and bronze at the 2025 Pacific Mini Games. The post Table Tennis first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Hambini Village’s journey to transformation
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Thanks to community unity, international support by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and visionary leadership, 9,000+ people of Hambini Village in East Sepik Province not only took a path to improve their economic prospects using cocoa but also paved the way for a br

item.title: NRL Commentators Impressed by PNG Chiefs Development Blueprint
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea’s push to establish a strong rugby league future has drawn praise from visiting NRL commentators, who were given an inside look at the country’s long-term development plans. NRL Commentators Impressed by PNG Chiefs Development Blueprint The group of Australian-based media personalit

item.title: Mothers of Sumkar in Madang honoured on Mother’s Day
item.description: [Post Courier] “Mothers are the quiet strength of our communities,” Tony Dibul, a Karkar Islander, said in a Mother’s Day message to Sumkar District in Madang Province. Mothers in Sumkar District, Madang Province, are being honoured today on Mother’s Day in a message that reflects on their daily struggles, strengt

item.title: Enga police apprehended prime suspect in relation to attempted murder
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] POLICE in Enga Province have apprehended the prime suspect in relation to the attempted murder of a young man at Kumbas Market in Wapenamanda District on Saturday 02nd May 2026. The suspect was arrested on Wednesday 6th May 2026 at Mt Hagen, Western Highlands Province, following coordinated operatio

item.title: Waigani Parish finalizes preparations as ordination of Deacon David Tiko Oala draws near
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Before the priesthood ordination of Deacon David Tiko Oala. O Carm, the Mary Queen of Pacific Parish in Waigani has completed final preparations for what is set to be one of its largest celebrations this year. Speaking to our reporter, Mathias Lasia said preparations began in March and spanned two m

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: ACT NOW goes to court to secure publication of information on the forest industry
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is taking legal action against the PNG Forest Authority to secure the release of information about large-scale logging operations.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: A day at Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kate Webster reports that recent renovations to Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery have revived a cultural heartbeat. The post A day at Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.description: [Education News PNG] 88676120 Download The post Call for Applications for Membership of the National Higher and Technical Education Board (NHTEB) first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier
item.description: [Inside PNG] The Autonomous Bougainville Government has launched Bougainville Wings Limited, describing it as Bougainville’s flag carrier and a milestone for the region’s aviation sector. The post ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier first appeared on Inside PNG . The post ABG launches Boug

item.title: O’Neill warns PNG risks flawed 2027 polls without urgent action
item.description: [PNG Facts] Concerns are growing that Papua New Guinea may head into the 2027 General Election unprepared, with former prime minister Peter O’Neill cautioning that delays and inaction could jeopardise the entire process. O’Neill warns PNG risks flawed 2027 polls without urgent action Speaking outside Parliament

item.title: Veteran teacher says motherhood is a calling that never ends
item.description: [Post Courier] For nearly four decades, Mrs Samela Pisep has stood in classrooms across Papua New Guinea, helping children learn, grow and believe in themselves. But beyond the chalkboards and lesson books, she says her greatest role has always been being a mother. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web

item.title: Marape Welcomes US Delegation for Second PNG–US Strategic Partnership Dialogue
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has welcomed the United States delegation led by Ambassador Michael G. DeSombre, United States Assistant Secretary of State for East Asian and Pacific Affairs, to PNG for the second Papua New Guinea–United States Strategic Partnership Dialogue currently taking place in Po

item.title: World’s largest Law Firm strengthens PNG disputes capability with Partner Gertrude Tamade
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Dentons, the world’s largest law firm, has strengthened its Australasian disputes capability with the promotion of Gertrude Tamade to Litigation Partner in its Papua New Guinea office. In a media statement, the company said Gertrude is a highly regarded senior litigation lawyer with more than 20 yea

item.title: Who's monitoring the social impacts of PNG's mining industry?
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The following article is about the social impacts of the mining industry in the country. According to the writer, inequality and environmental damage remain stubborn features of the sector although Papua New Guinea has never lacked ideas about how to manage these impacts.

item.title: Independence 1 September 2027 :Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting
item.description: [Bougainville News] Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting Sir Jerry Mateparae, Distinguished Moderator, the Honourable James Marape, Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, Foreign Missions, distinguished dignitaries, ladies and gentlemen. item.title: Six questions about greylisting that every Papua New Guinean business exec should be asking
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In February, the Financial Action Task Force added Papua New Guinea to its “grey list” of jurisdictions under increased monitoring. In this exclusive opinion piece for Business Advantage PNG , John Burke, an expert in AML/CTF compliance for PNG, shares how businesses can maintain their banking relat

item.description: [Education News PNG] APIA, SAMOA – 16 February 2026 – The Pacific Academy of Sciences (PAS) today announced two historic milestones for Pacific research and innovation at its The post Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: ABG to resume Panguna landowner consultations following protest
item.description: [Inside PNG] ABG resumes Panguna landowner talks Monday to address EL01 license grievances and boost transparency following recent protests and cyclone delays. The post ABG to resume Panguna landowner consultations following protest first appeared on Inside PNG . The post ABG to resume Panguna landowner consulta

item.title: PNG Chiefs Secure NRL Star Alex Johnston for 2028 Season
item.description: [PNG Facts] In a move expected to energise rugby league in Papua New Guinea, Kumuls representative Alex Johnston has signed on to join the PNG Chiefs in 2028, adding further firepower to the club’s growing ambitions. The veteran winger, known for his remarkable try-scoring ability, holds the record as the NRL’s

item.title: Sir Peter Ipatas commits K7 million to revitalise Porgera healthcare and education
item.description: [Post Courier] The reopening of Paiam Hospital in the mining township of Porgera has become a top priority, with the Enga Provincial Government committing K4 million toward comprehensive renovation and rehabilitation works. Porgera Development Authority Manager, Mr Stephen Kikala, highlighted a recent visit by vet

item.title: Public Voting Opens for People’s Choice Award Category
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By DALCY LULUA PUBLIC voting has officially opened for the People’s Choice Award at the SP Sports Awards 2026, with fans across Papua New Guinea invited to have their say via FM100, SMS and WhatsApp before voting closes on May 15. The PNG Sports Foundation has reaffirmed its commitment to community-

item.title: Teacher injured in solar installation accident calls for urgent health, education support in Kutubu
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A primary school teacher in Kutubu, Southern Highlands Province, has issued a strong appeal to major resource companies and government leaders following a workplace accident that exposed serious gaps in basic services for local communities. Mr. Benjamin Okene, a teacher at Kopi Waterfront Primary Sc

item.title: ACT NOW goes to court seeking publication of information on the forest industry
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is taking legal action against the PNG Forest Authority to secure the release of information about large-scale logging operations.

item.title: PNG PM Orders Probe into K20m Defence Deal
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has ordered an immediate review into a reported K20 million transaction involving the PNG Defence Force, directing Chief Secretary Ivan Pomaleo to investigate the matter thoroughly. The Prime Minister said the issue only came to his attention after it was

item.title: Building a Nation on Land and People
item.description: [Act Now PNG] In 1974, as Papua New Guinea prepared for Self-Government and Independence, the country faced an important choice. It could continue with the colonial system, central control, foreign companies, and fast economic growth that mainly benefited outsiders. Or it could choose a different path.

item.title: PNG PM Marape Sends Strong Message Ahead of 2027 Polls
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has set out a national direction prioritising governance, institutional strength and leadership renewal as the country approaches the 2027 National General Election. PNG Focus on Governance as Marape Shifts Attention from Early Election Talk He said the G
